steps.title: Find the original y-intercept
steps.content:
To find where the graph of $9x + 10y = 19$ crosses the $y$-axis, set $x = 0$:
$$10y = 19$$
$$y = \frac{19}{10}$$

steps.title: Apply the vertical shift
steps.content:
A vertical shift up 6 units adds 6 to every $y$-coordinate. The new $y$-intercept is:
$$\frac{19}{10} + 6 = \frac{19}{10} + \frac{60}{10} = \frac{79}{10}$$
This matches choice A — A is correct.
